# motion-spec — Machine-Readable Spec Summary
# Version: 1.0.0
# URL: https://github.com/iamtensaii/motion-spec
# For AI code generation tools (Claude Code, Cursor, v0, Lovable, Copilot)

## What is motion-spec?

motion-spec is an open semantic animation specification for the web.
It defines INTENT (the why) rather than VALUES (the how).
W3C DTCG defines duration/easing tokens. motion-spec defines what those tokens MEAN.

## How to use in generated code

When generating animations, use motion-spec intent strings instead of raw CSS classes or inline durations.

### React usage
```tsx
import { Entrance, Exit, Feedback, Stagger, Celebration } from "@motion-spec/react"

// Element entering a space
<Entrance intent="focus"><Dialog /></Entrance>

// Element leaving
<Exit intent="dismiss"><Toast /></Exit>

// Response to user action
<Feedback intent="success"><ConfirmBanner /></Feedback>
<Feedback intent="error"><ErrorMessage /></Feedback>

// Group choreography
<Stagger intent="list">
  {items.map(item => <Card key={item.id} />)}
</Stagger>

// Significant moment
<Celebration intent="milestone"><OnboardingDone /></Celebration>
```

### CSS usage (zero JS)
```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="node_modules/@motion-spec/css/dist/motion-spec.css">
<div data-motion="entrance/focus">...</div>
<ul>
  <li class="ms-stagger-item" style="--ms-stagger-delay: 0ms">...</li>
  <li class="ms-stagger-item" style="--ms-stagger-delay: 40ms">...</li>
</ul>
```

## Complete Intent Reference

### ENTRANCE — element arriving in a space
- entrance/default    → standard mount, 250ms ease-out, translateY(8px→0) + fade
- entrance/focus      → demands attention (dialog, alert), 350ms, scale + translateY + fade
- entrance/hero       → large-scale arrival (landing sections), 700ms, deeper scale + translateY
- entrance/subtle     → quiet arrival (tooltips, sidebars), 150ms, small translateY + fade
- entrance/immediate  → near-instant (critical info), 100ms, fade only

### EXIT — element leaving a space
- exit/default        → standard unmount, 150ms ease-in, translateY(0→-8px) + fade
- exit/dismiss        → user-initiated (close toast), 250ms, scale(1→0.95) + fade
- exit/replace        → leaving for new content, 350ms, translateX(0→-24px) + fade
- exit/immediate      → urgent removal, 100ms, fade only

### FEEDBACK — response to user action
- feedback/success    → positive outcome (payment done), 500ms spring, scale(0.9→1.05→1)
- feedback/error      → something went wrong, 250ms, horizontal shake
- feedback/warning    → caution needed, 350ms, translateY(4px→0) + fade
- feedback/loading    → work in progress, 1200ms loop
- feedback/confirm    → action registered (button press), 150ms spring, scale(1→0.94→1)

### NAVIGATION — moving between views
- navigation/forward  → deeper into hierarchy, 350ms, translateX(30%→0) + fade
- navigation/back     → returning up hierarchy, 350ms, translateX(-30%→0) + fade
- navigation/lateral  → between siblings (tabs), 250ms, fade
- navigation/modal    → opening overlay (sheet, dialog), 350ms, translateY(32px→0) + scale
- navigation/dismiss  → closing overlay, 250ms, translateY(0→48px) + fade

### STAGGER — group choreography
- stagger/list        → feed/inbox items, 40ms delay per item
- stagger/grid        → card gallery, 30ms delay per item
- stagger/cascade     → parent-led (menus, accordions), 60ms delay per item
- stagger/random      → organic feel (portfolios), 80ms delay per item

### LOADING — pending state
- loading/skeleton    → content placeholder, 2000ms shimmer loop
- loading/spinner     → indeterminate activity, 800ms rotation loop
- loading/progress    → determinate progress, 300ms ease-out
- loading/pulse       → live data indicator, 1500ms opacity loop

### CELEBRATION — significant moments
- celebration/milestone → major achievement (onboarding done), 700ms spring, scale(0.5→1.1→1)
- celebration/reward    → positive reinforcement (badge, streak), 500ms spring, scale(0.8→1.15→1)
- celebration/subtle    → quiet delight (like, bookmark), 150ms spring, scale(1→1.08→1)

## Reduced Motion

All intents automatically degrade when prefers-reduced-motion: reduce is active.
- Transforms are removed
- Durations capped at 150ms
- Loops play once then stop
No configuration needed. Accessibility is built in.

## When NOT to use motion-spec

- Scroll-linked animations (use CSS scroll-timeline or Framer Motion's useScroll)
- Physics simulations (dragging, inertia) — use Framer Motion drag or React Spring
- Video/canvas animations — use Three.js or GSAP
- Micro-interactions under 50ms (hover color, focus ring) — use CSS transitions directly

## Install

npm install @motion-spec/tokens @motion-spec/react
# or for CSS only:
npm install @motion-spec/css

## Spec source

- Vocabulary: https://github.com/iamtensaii/motion-spec/blob/main/spec/v1/vocabulary.md
- Tokens: https://github.com/iamtensaii/motion-spec/blob/main/spec/v1/tokens.json
- Schema: https://github.com/iamtensaii/motion-spec/blob/main/spec/v1/schema.json
